_metadata.title: Florida Jurisdictional Asset Protection — Citadel HQ Move Analysis
sources: FL Constitution Art. VII §5 (no income tax)
Citadel X-17A-5 FY2022 vs FY2023 cover pages

FY2023.address: 200 S Biscayne Blvd, Suite 3300, Miami, FL 33131

FY2024.address: 200 S Biscayne Blvd, Suite 3300, Miami, FL 33131

layer_1_tax.calculation: Based on ~$4.2B FY2024 net income × (13.3% NY + 3.876% NYC surcharge)

layer_2_homestead.key_feature: No dollar cap on property value (unlike TX which has acreage limits only)
layer_2_homestead.residency_requirement: Bona fide FL residency, intent to remain

layer_3_tbe.protection: Tenancy by the entirety for all personal property
layer_3_tbe.legal_basis: FL Statute §689.115; Beal Bank v. Almand (2001)
layer_3_tbe.scope: Stocks, bank accounts, brokerage holdings — all personal property
layer_3_tbe.effect: Individual creditor of one spouse cannot seize jointly held assets

layer_4_ucc.reason: Securities perfected by control (UCC §8-106), not filing. Filing jurisdiction shifts to FL for personal guarantees only.

layer_5_regulatory.explanation: FINRA, SEC, OCC regulation is federal. State of domicile does not affect regulatory jurisdiction for broker-dealers.
